hero.title: Software
hero.subtitle: 4+ years shipping scalable web & mobile products
hero.description: I build end-to-end software — from polished UIs to APIs in the cloud. React, TypeScript, Django, and AWS are my day-to-day stack. I care about performance, maintainability, and craft.

projects.title: Selected Work
projects.subtitle: Live products and open-source projects reflecting real-world impact
projects.githubEmptySubtitle: Check out my GitHub profile for experiments, side projects, and open-source contributions.
projects.privateAppNote: The live product is account-based; the images below are real UI from the platform so you can see the work without a login.

eva-protect.lead: EVA Protect is an InsurTech platform for personal and asset protection. As co-founder and CTO, I owned the technical side end to end: AWS infrastructure, a Next.js and TypeScript product, integrations, and the experiences both internal teams and users rely on daily. Because production access is gated, this case study replaces a generic “visit site” link with real screens from the app—so the scope of the build is visible without an account.
highlights: End-to-end ownership from cloud architecture to shipped UI
Full-stack delivery: frontend, APIs, deployment, and operational hygiene
Responsive product: desktop workflows and mobile-critical journeys in one codebase
Built for operations—not a landing page: dashboards, data density, and clarity under load

galleryCaptions.dashboardDesktop: Operations dashboard (desktop)—core overview and control surfaces for day-to-day work.
galleryCaptions.dashboardMobile: Same product on mobile: layout and density tuned for small screens and touch.
galleryCaptions.gpsDesktop: Location-aware flow (desktop)—maps and geo context integrated into the product journey.
galleryCaptions.gpsMobile: Field-oriented mobile view: GPS and map UI where on-the-go access matters.

yayo-photo-film.lead: A live photographer portfolio and booking-oriented site: public-facing gallery, fast loads, and a practical split between what visitors see and what the photographer can maintain without redeploying the whole site.
highlights: Built with Astro for a lightweight, content-first public experience and selective interactivity where it matters
Supabase behind the scenes for storing and serving media—structured assets instead of hard-coding every image path
Dedicated manager-style area to update gallery content and keep the site fresh without touching production code on each change
Clear information architecture: showcase work first, then guide visitors toward booking or contact
Deployed and tuned for real traffic on Vercel—performance and UX treated as part of the product, not an afterthought

pokeshop.lead: A storefront-style front end built to demonstrate deliberate client-side state: how to cache list data, keep per-Pokémon details handy, and derive things like pricing from that state so the UI stays fast and consistent as users explore the catalog.
highlights: React Context as the app’s state layer—favorites, cached API payloads, and UI-derived values live in one predictable place
In-memory cache for listing data so revisiting grids or toggling views doesn’t spam the API or flash empty states
Per-species bookkeeping: when you open a Pokémon, costs and related figures are tied to that entity in state (not scattered local variables)
Favorites flow: add/remove with immediate UI feedback while the underlying context keeps the list in sync across screens
Vite + TypeScript: small, readable modules for actions, context, and views—easy to extend with filters, carts, or persistence later

experience.title: Experience
experience.subtitle: From industrial automation to full-stack software — a path built on systems thinking

highlights: Hotel platforms: reservations, billing, and day-to-day operations for hospitality clients.
Real-time water monitoring for utilities — consumption tracking and anomaly detection.
AWS (S3, RDS, EC2) for reliable environments and deployments.
Performance-minded web apps and REST APIs, frontend to backend.
Internal tools and admin dashboards with React, Redux, TypeScript, and Ant Design.
Modular Django REST APIs and CRUD for internal data.
Agile delivery with fast iteration and clear ownership.
Internal apps with React, Redux, and TypeScript — responsive, data-heavy UIs.
Real-time features with Socket.io and GraphQL subscriptions.
Google Maps and META integrations; DB performance with Sequelize and Prisma.
AWS pipelines (S3, RDS, EC2, CodePipeline) and React Native apps.
Partnered with product engineering on shock absorber specs — tight collaboration between design and execution.
Built monitoring solutions for pneumatic and hydraulic equipment (temperature, pressure, leak detection).
Programmed and tuned FANUC welding robots — quick to adopt new industrial tooling.
Supported project engineering in a carbon fiber manufacturing environment — specs, schedules, and stakeholder follow-up.
Helped prepare technical documentation and track deliverables alongside senior engineers.
Rotated through plant areas to understand production, quality, and how engineering decisions hit the floor.
Built a solid base in industrial teamwork before moving into full-time project engineering roles.

skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: Technologies I work with every day and tools I've shipped to production
skills.description: Continuously expanding my toolkit with emerging technologies and best practices. Each project presents an opportunity to deepen these capabilities.

about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: Engineer. Builder. Co-founder.
about.description1: I'm a Software Engineer based in Guadalajara, México, with 4+ years building web and mobile applications. I lean toward frontend with React and TypeScript, and I'm just as comfortable shipping Django APIs and cloud infrastructure.
about.education: I earned my degree in Mechanical and Electrical Engineering (Ingeniería Mecánica Eléctrica) from Universidad de Guadalajara — a foundation that still shapes how I think about systems, constraints, and problems in the physical world.
about.description2: I've worked in environments ranging from high-growth startups to enterprise teams at companies like IntouchCX, where I built real-time systems, AWS-deployed platforms, and cross-platform mobile apps.
about.description3: Outside of code, I enjoy exploring new tools, contributing to open-source, and pushing the quality bar on everything I ship.

cofounder.description: Insurance technology platform focused on personal and asset protection. I co-founded EVA Protect and lead all technical decisions — from architecture and infrastructure to product and team.

about.philosophyText: I believe clean, well-structured code and thoughtful UX are not opposites — they reinforce each other. I build with scalability in mind from day one, and I pay attention to the details that turn good software into great software.
